#### Accepted Filter Paragraph

> White Noise: Nostalgia from the 1960s
> skip to main |
> skip to sidebar
> Halfway between the gutter and the stars
> Friday, September 16, 2011
> Nostalgia from the 1960s
> A blast from my past. A&P grocery stores, which we had in Canada too for a while, sold Jane Parker bread. I distinctly remember the cellophane wrapping around the loaf, kept closed by the paper label glued on at the ends.There was a time in my youth, when I was around 5 or 6, when I so enjoyed a loaf fresh bread, that I would feast on it - plain - right out of the wrapper. It was so good! Maybe they spiked it with something back then, who knows.A&P was bought out by the Quebec grocery chain 'Metro' in 2005. For all Western Canadians who've never heard of A&P, the chain didn't make it out west. Check out that snazzy font on the logo.....
> Blog'd by
> Karl Plesz
> at
> 5:41 PM
> 1 comment:
> ANDY PAGE
> said...
> Thanks for the memories.I am thankful that I can still enjoy Jane Parker fruitcake during the holidays.But JP white bread, and the other 32 varieties like ryes and buttermilk, and cinnamon bread were favorites as well.I just learned that Great A&P will be rebranding all of their bakery product except Jane Parker fruit cake with the name, "two forks".

#### Accepted Filter Paragraph

> My Forlorn Corn Field.
> My Forlorn Corn Field.
> Meow. Submit Me My name is Josh Leeson.I have been living for 20 years. I am being educated at Ohio University. I have a passion for theater, dance, pop music, fashion, and outer space. I always have so much to say, but I can never seem to fully express myself. However, the defining idea or concept my scattered and unorganized mind results in is this universal mentality of a celestial like place where I can be serene, comforted, safe, free, loved, and unconditionally happy. This place is a not just my fantasy but my goal. I am determined to find such a place, and achieve such a satisfaction in life. Despite how delusional this may seem, I feel that what I want in my overall life transcends most things everyone else wants...its just that mine is manifested in a more romantic like way. I believe that everything I do in my life is slowly pointing me towards this ultimate destination, this ultimate goal. The If you want to know more about me, just ask! :)
> (via kashief)

> My Forlorn Corn Field.

> Meow. Submit Me My name is Josh Leeson.I have been living for 20 years. I am being educated at Ohio University. I have a passion for theater, dance, pop music, fashion, and outer space. I always have so much to say, but I can never seem to fully express myself. However, the defining idea or concept my scattered and unorganized mind results in is this universal mentality of a celestial like place where I can be serene, comforted, safe, free, loved, and unconditionally happy. This place is a not just my fantasy but my goal. I am determined to find such a place, and achieve such a satisfaction in life. Despite how delusional this may seem, I feel that what I want in my overall life transcends most things everyone else wants...its just that mine is manifested in a more romantic like way. I believe that everything I do in my life is slowly pointing me towards this ultimate destination, this ultimate goal. The If you want to know more about me, just ask! :)

#### Accepted Filter Paragraph

> Years my father nostalgic for my mother.
> She'd set the Shabbat table
> Guess the white cloth the flu vegetable land
>  Let the fine flour, put the meat
> Put the tomatoes and garlic
> She could cook.
> It tasted feed came from us
> And so would put us as cookies Yu Yu Tonisaiot
> Sweet biscuits dipped in liquid dam
> Rosewater would add my grandmother if she had been allowed to
> But here in my mother would make the salads very thinly
> To unite us at the Shabbat table.

> Kitchen

> Years my father nostalgic for my mother.
> She'd set the Shabbat table
> Guess the white cloth the flu vegetable land
>  Let the fine flour, put the meat
> Put the tomatoes and garlic
> She could cook.
> It tasted feed came from us
> And so would put us as cookies Yu Yu Tonisaiot
> Sweet biscuits dipped in liquid dam
> Rosewater would add my grandmother if she had been allowed to
> But here in my mother would make the salads very thinly
> To unite us at the Shabbat table.

> I did not learn a foreign language parents
> My parents speak their language changes
> Request a sweet Polish father
> Tunisian mature mother
> And I did not learn of them a foreign language
> Only Hebrew sucked mother that it was not her mother tongue
> She spoke with her sisters in French
> Arabic
> Jewish and jargon that far.

> In the kitchen I did not learn a foreign language
> Only Hebrew

- **Seed Score:** 0.451

- **Matches Export References:** `matches_en.md` #3229
- **Keywords:** `ancestry`
- **Nearest Semantic Reference (Not a Summary):** 'Being part of the diaspora means I am caught between cultures, always longing for a home that may no longer exist as I remember.'
- **Extraction Method:** deterministic source-paragraph selection; no generated text
- **Source URL:** [http://avriel.tumblr.com/tagged/biracial](http://avriel.tumblr.com/tagged/biracial)
- **Capture Count:** 1
- **Crawl Dataset:** `CC-MAIN-2013-20`
- **Source File:** `crawl-data/CC-MAIN-2013-20/segments/1368697772439/wet/CC-MAIN-20130516094932-00096-ip-10-60-113-184.ec2.internal.warc.wet.gz`

#### Accepted Filter Paragraph

> Avriel
> Avriel ask archive rss answers music me about for press & booking inquiries email avrielbooking@gmail.com
> I just wanna say, I read your "Implications of Being Biracial" post and I'm so glad I'm not the only one who feels that way. I was constantly trying to find something to cling to as a definition of myself. I even tried to convince myself for years that I was "really" colombian. And then I realized I don't have anything, and it was the loneliest feeling in the world. When it comes down to it, I'm just a girl from California. My beginning is here and so is my end. So thanks. (hope this isnt weird)
> by cristinanina
> Thank you so much for sending this message! We’re all connected in our desires to belong, but once you realize (as I believe you have and as I have recently) that you are magnificently beautiful and perfect as you are, life begins to feel so pleasant.It’s always nice to understand where we have come from, but it is so much more important — so much more powerful — to know that we have ultimate control over who we are right now and who we will become. Our past, our ancestry, our lineage does not define or bind us. And right now, you and I are defining what it means to be mixed. We are reaffirming an idea that to be American, to be Black, to be Columbian, to be Jewish, to be biracial, or to be _____________ can mean so many things and it’s silly to try and define these concepts so strictly.Just by living our beautifully complex lives and connecting with each other (even if it’s just on tumblr) we are having such a profound impact on the collective consciousness. I think that’s pretty cool
> Asked 1 year ago ST

> a not her home
> I don’t call myself as a collector, I don’t want to be focused on collecting, nor to become a robocameratoolgearmindfreak. I just simply liked/loved them, and try to use every single one of them as my daily partner. Then I try to fix them as much as I can (Lots of great people out there who show you how to do it by the way). Most of the time I get them from the flea market. It’s such a great feeling when you finally have them and to take it home with you.

> not so mini mini portrait.
> i met this kind man in my home town. we met several times and did a nice quality chat overtime. In the end he gave me a few of his cameras, in this is one of them. - **Source File:** `crawl-data/CC-MAIN-2013-20/segments/1368697772439/wet/CC-MAIN-20130516094932-00037-ip-10-60-113-184.ec2.internal.warc.wet.gz`

#### Accepted Filter Paragraph

> Favourite childhood memories?
> Favourite childhood memories?
> greenspun.com : LUSENET : zootzie : One Thread
> Come on, spill it. Those good times that make you feel all warm and squishy inside ;)
> -- Bon bon (zootzie@yahoo.com), May 20, 2000
> Answers
> I've already mentioned this in my journal, but thinking of Papa Hemingway as my grandpa was always kind of nice.
> -- Siobhan (zootzie@yahoo.com), May 20, 2000.
> Hmm ... I don't know if these are my favourites necessarily, but the first ones I thought of were going to Disneyworld when I was 3, and how me and my little brother would get to chill in my parents' room before we went to bed at night, watching cartoons my parents had taped for us like the Muppet Babies and stuff. So I wasn't cultured enough to be kickin it with Hemingway when I was 5 :P lol
> -- Jamie (starvingartiste@yahoo.com), May 20, 2000.
> hmmm... its hard to think of just one favorite childhood memory... but one that comes to mind is the first time i went to new york when i was about 5 or 6. we went on a helicoptor tour of manhattan and flew around the statue of liberty and then we took the ferry over and went inside the statue of liberty and took pictures. It was a long time ago and i was very young so most of the memories are more like hazy photographs... i remember a fancy restaurant... a burger king... a carnival-type place... my aunt's apartment with the big mirror with all the showbiz lights around it... a snoopy windup toy (which i still have)... and a statue of liberty piggy bank. Its all blurred together in my memory today... except for the helicoptor ride... i remember the helicoptor ride like it was yesterday. i believe it was my first time ever in a flying machine... so i was just wide-eyed with excitement. sigh... now i want to go to new york...
> -- mike (supermike1999@yahoo.com), May 21, 2000.
> My childhood memories are pretty simple. I like to remember the good ole days of watching my brothers playing baseball and me gawking at the hotties on their teams. I like to remember when I used to play softball. I used to be really good at it. I also love to remember me taking dance classes and tapping all around just to annoy my brothers. My friends and I used to make up dances to everything, everyday after school. C&C Music Factory or Janet Jackson. Awwjeah. Once when there was a huge storm, my brother, cousins, and a few friends of ours put our swim suits on and went swimming in the puddles. (which weren't even that big) Ahh, memories..
> -- Kristin (akris10@bombdiggity.com), May 21, 2000.
> One of my earliest and best memories is of walking
> my grandmother's dog, out around her Orange County
> neighborhood, at about 8 or 9. It was very peaceful
> out there, hardly any traffic, and no people except
> for the occasional jogger. The weather was perfect
> and my mental state was bliss. Maybe I didn't know
> everything back then, but it sure felt that way.
> -- Paul (gilbreathfamily@worldnet.att.net), August 11, 2000.
> Moderation questions? read the FAQ

#### Accepted Filter Paragraph

> Kids Games
> Kids GamesA Collection of the Classic and Modern
> I have a soft place in my heart for kids games. Who doesn't remember some of their favorites while growing up? There was tag, duck duck goose, pin the tail on the donkey and many more classics when I was young. In this collection, you will not only find some of the classics, but lots more kids games to thrill and excite the children on your guest list.
> Party Tip:Don't be afraid to blend your themes. It's okay to have a "pirate ship" sink to the bottom of Bikini Bottom and have SpongeBob and Patrick help find the treasure. Thinking outside the box can bring a freshness to the party and open up more avenues for decoration.
> Categories Available
> Relay RacesBalloon GamesCircle GamesPreschool GamesKids Indoor GamesKids Outdoor GamesKids Scavenger HuntsFrisbee GamesKids Party Game Index A - Z
> New! Comments Have your say about this page! Leave me a comment in the box below.
> Kids Games: The Most Popular Ones
> Article Source: Ezine Articles.comBy nature, kids are the ones who are really into playing games. During this stage of growth, they do not think of anything that's really serious. In other words, they see things in a very simple way. They are not yet aware of the benefits associated with playing games and if they are getting anything good from it. They just want to spend time with their friends and have fun under whether they are under the sun or under the rain. There are so many types of games that kids play from the traditional games to the modern ones. Because of the advent of computers and the internet, many kids are going with the option of playing online games. Although these games help in developing their planning and thinking skills, those that require physical movements are still better. Here are some of the most popular games that are really good for kids.Hide and seek is of the most played and is among the most popular games that kids play. In fact, almost everyone in this world was able to play this game during their childhood days. This is very simple and easy game to play, as its name suggests, one kid will be the one to seek and the others will hide. The "it" will close his eyes facing the tree or a wall which will serve as the base while the other kids will find a place to hide. After counting 1-20 or what was agreed, the "it" starts to seek for the other players. The "it" should do his best to tag every player before the reach the base and the first player that gets tagged before touching the base will be the next "it".Another game that many kids play is the washer toss game which will require a cup, a box and some washers. The box and the cup should be placed at the center of the playing area. Kids will then take turns in tossing the washers to the box and the cup. When a washer lands inside the box, it is equivalent to 1 point and when it lands in the cup, it will be 3 points. The first player to reach or exceed 21 points will be declared as the winner. There is also a game that is somewhat similar to this game, the cornhole game. Some people actually call it as a bean bag toss depending on their country. The same idea applies when kids are playing washer toss, kids will aim for a hole on the board using bean bags and the player to first reach 21 points score will be the winner.Aside from these games, kick ball, red light green light, hop scotch, dodge ball and shadowing are also among the most popular games that children play. All these games are definitely very simple and easy to play but the happiness and joy that the games bring to the kids is absolutely great. Playing these games has indeed become a part of every kid's life.William G. Steinmetz enjoys writing for TargetToTossGames.com which sells cornhole game and washer toss game as well as a host of additional products.

> Kids Games
> Kids GamesA Collection of the Classic and Modern
> I have a soft place in my heart for kids games. Who doesn't remember some of their favorites while growing up? There was tag, duck duck goose, pin the tail on the donkey and many more classics when I was young. In this collection, you will not only find some of the classics, but lots more kids games to thrill and excite the children on your guest list.
> Party Tip:Don't be afraid to blend your themes. It's okay to have a "pirate ship" sink to the bottom of Bikini Bottom and have SpongeBob and Patrick help find the treasure. Thinking outside the box can bring a freshness to the party and open up more avenues for decoration.

> Kids Games: The Most Popular Ones
> Article Source: Ezine Articles.comBy nature, kids are the ones who are really into playing games. During this stage of growth, they do not think of anything that's really serious. In other words, they see things in a very simple way. They are not yet aware of the benefits associated with playing games and if they are getting anything good from it. They just want to spend time with their friends and have fun under whether they are under the sun or under the rain. There are so many types of games that kids play from the traditional games to the modern ones. Because of the advent of computers and the internet, many kids are going with the option of playing online games. Although these games help in developing their planning and thinking skills, those that require physical movements are still better. Here are some of the most popular games that are really good for kids.Hide and seek is of the most played and is among the most popular games that kids play. In fact, almost everyone in this world was able to play this game during their childhood days. This is very simple and easy game to play, as its name suggests, one kid will be the one to seek and the others will hide. The "it" will close his eyes facing the tree or a wall which will serve as the base while the other kids will find a place to hide. After counting 1-20 or what was agreed, the "it" starts to seek for the other players. The "it" should do his best to tag every player before the reach the base and the first player that gets tagged before touching the base will be the next "it".Another game that many kids play is the washer toss game which will require a cup, a box and some washers. The box and the cup should be placed at the center of the playing area. Kids will then take turns in tossing the washers to the box and the cup. When a washer lands inside the box, it is equivalent to 1 point and when it lands in the cup, it will be 3 points. The first player to reach or exceed 21 points will be declared as the winner. There is also a game that is somewhat similar to this game, the cornhole game. Some people actually call it as a bean bag toss depending on their country. The same idea applies when kids are playing washer toss, kids will aim for a hole on the board using bean bags and the player to first reach 21 points score will be the winner.Aside from these games, kick ball, red light green light, hop scotch, dodge ball and shadowing are also among the most popular games that children play. All these games are definitely very simple and easy to play but the happiness and joy that the games bring to the kids is absolutely great. Playing these games has indeed become a part of every kid's life.William G. Steinmetz enjoys writing for TargetToTossGames.com which sells cornhole game and washer toss game as well as a host of additional products.

> childhood memory
> The homes of your childhood
> 23 July, 2011
> How do you feel about the homes you have lived in?
> Were you an ‘armed forces kid’ who saw a succession of different houses every couple years, barely remembering some of them? A third generation farm kid who saw your grandparent’s hard work in every board and shingle? An inner city child who lived in cookie-cutter apartments?
> Memories of houses are percolating in my head as I prepare to leave the one that has been home for over 20 years. That’s longer than I lived in my childhood home (Google Street View below), yet the childhood home still holds a stronger emotional pull.
> A home’s central role in the emotional landscape of our lives is most evident when we reflect upon our formative years. Childhood homes trigger great emotional outpourings because they are the sites where we first dream, imagine, and play. A home’s interior is, in the words of French philosopher Gaston Bachelard, “the human being’s first world.” Tags: childhood
